narrative:
Clouds and wet weather will keep temperatures down a bit this weekend, but it will be muggy. Occasional showers and thunderstorms develop both today and Sunday, but there will be some dry hours as well, especially today. A few storms this weekend could drop a lot of rain in a short time along with some gusty winds. Warmer weather with more sunshine and fewer showers returns next week.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is here and being delivered to our retail outlets. Thanks for a record-breaking pre-publication sale! You can still avoid shipping costs with our pickup events beginning Thursday, July 2, in Boone. We'll take that to Banner Elk, West Jefferson, Spruce Pine, and Black Mountain next week; click here for details. You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page . Check out volume discounts on the calendar and the "Mix and Match" sale on Drinkware.

Have Backup Indoor Weekend Plans

Last Updated Sat July 11 at 10:31AM by Wayne Presnell

Clouds and wet weather will keep temperatures down a bit this weekend, but it will be muggy. Occasional showers and thunderstorms develop both today and Sunday, but there will be some dry hours as well, especially today. A few storms this weekend could drop a lot of rain in a short time along with some gusty winds. Warmer weather with more sunshine and fewer showers returns next week.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is here and being delivered to our retail outlets. Thanks for a record-breaking pre-publication sale! You can still avoid shipping costs with our pickup events beginning Thursday, July 2, in Boone. We'll take that to Banner Elk, West Jefferson, Spruce Pine, and Black Mountain next week; click here for details. You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page . Forecast Discussion

Weak low pressure at the surface and aloft over the mid-Mississippi Valley meanders southeast into the Ohio and Tennessee Valleys this weekend. The low pulls a front south toward our region, and sends waves of moisture to make for a fairly wet weekend. We want to stress that this won't be a complete washout, but you'll need to keep an eye to the sky and have backup indoor plans, especially Sunday. A few batches of showers and thunderstorms roll in from the west, or develop in our vicinity, just about all weekend.

There will be a tendency for activity to be more active in the morning through early afternoon today, then there may be a bit of a lull. On Sunday, it's more difficult to try to pin down a drier period, but activity may favor the afternoon and evening hours. Be aware that there's plenty of moisture and instability for some hefty downpours and strong gusty winds in the more vigorous storms.

The overall pattern continues into Monday and Tuesday, but the low pulls westward farther away from our area, and instability wanes. Clouds will keep temperatures a little cooler on Monday, but then high pressure reestablishes over the Gulf Coast states starting about Tuesday. We will return to more typical afternoon widely-separated showers and thundershowers that fade away each evening. Less cloud coverage and more sunshine spell a return to very warm weather once again.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Jul 08, 2026
Monday Message for July 6, 2026

It's the first Monday Message of July! Mercifully, temperatures are cooler this week, although rainfall continues to be scattered and somewhat unreliable. We also take a look back at June rainfall and temperatures.

Jul 06, 2026
Monday Message for June 29, 2026

It's the Independence Day / America 250 edition of the Monday Message, available to subscribers. This week, it's all about heat, humidity and isolated afternoon thunderstorms.
